tool_args.label: 8th Avenue Line CBTC
tool_args.raw_text: The project will provide CBTC from 59 St in Manhattan through High St in Brooklyn. Also includes providing CBTC equipment to existing R179 vehicles and CBTC equipment to support manufacturing of R211 vehicles. The 8 Av line has three services (A, C, E) and carries more than 710,000 daily riders (pre-COVID).

payload.description: CBTC project on the 8th Avenue Line from 59 St in Manhattan through High St in Brooklyn. Includes CBTC equipment for R179 and R211 vehicles. Budget $774M. SC January 2025. Contractor: LK Comstock. Approximately 57% complete.

tool_args.label: Queens Boulevard Line West CBTC (QBL-W)
tool_args.raw_text: The project provides CBTC from Union Turnpike in Queens through 50 St/8 Av on the 53 St line and 21 St/Queensbridge on the 63 St Line. The project has already placed CBTC into service on all sections, with the last section having been placed into operation February 2022. ATS-B is in-service.

payload.description: CBTC from Union Turnpike in Queens through 50 St/8 Av on 53 St line and 21 St/Queensbridge on 63 St Line. First interoperable CBTC project for NYCT, allowing trains with CBTC to run on same line with equipment from Siemens and Thales. Budget $666M, EAC $729M. LK Comstock 88% complete (SC July 2022), Siemens 95% complete (SC Dec 2022), Thales 88% complete (SC June 2022).

tool_args.label: Culver Line CBTC
tool_args.raw_text: Culver Line signal modernization will improve reliability and resiliency of service between W 8 St and Church Ave in Brooklyn. The new signaling system will employ CBTC and add three new signal facilities at Ditmas Av, Bay Pkwy, and Avenue X. 4.7 route miles, 12 subway stations.

payload.description: CBTC between W 8 St and Church Ave in Brooklyn. 4.7 route miles, 12 subway stations. 3 new signal facilities at Ditmas Av, Bay Pkwy, and Avenue X. Single combined contract: Tutor Perini (installer) with Siemens (CBTC supplier). Budget $482M (unchanged). SC baseline Aug 2022, latest update Nov 2023.

tool_args.label: Queens Boulevard Line East CBTC (QBL-E)
tool_args.raw_text: The project will provide CBTC systems from north of Union Turnpike to 179 St Station on the QBL (Hillside Av) Line (F). The new signal system shall be CBTC with SSI and ATS. Axle Counter Systems shall replace track circuits north of Union Turnpike Station to 179 St Station.

payload.description: CBTC from north of Union Turnpike to 179 St Station on Hillside Avenue Line (F). Includes modernization of 4 interlockings (179 St, 169 St, Parsons Blvd, Briarwood). Budget $539.5M. SC Q2 2026. Installer: EJ Electric ($440.5M). CBTC Supplier: Mitsubishi ($98M). DCS: Siemens (in negotiation). First CBTC project adding Mitsubishi to interoperability.

tool_args.label: ISIM-B Module 3
tool_args.raw_text: The program is to provide the systems and subsystems to build out the new South Wall in the centralized RCC, which includes a Large-Scale Display and 41 QBL workstations. The project also includes health status indications for signal devices for the MOW/Signals Central Monitoring System.

payload.description: Systems and subsystems for new South Wall in Rail Control Center (RCC), including Large-Scale Display and 41 QBL workstations. Budget $154M. SC December 2022. Scope substantially descoped as NYCT needs changed.

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: Overall, the project is 70% complete, well within budget at $336M and remains on schedule for Substantial Completion (SC) in November 2022.

payload.project_name: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement
payload.description: Orthotropic deck replacement at Throgs Neck Bridge. 70% complete, within budget at $336M, on schedule for SC November 2022. EAC $310M. Early construction issues being mitigated by resequencing. Minor scope transfer of suspender rope and main cable work to future project.

tool_args.label: Coney Island Yard Project
tool_args.raw_text: Overall, the project is 80% complete, within the $521M budget, and the revised SC date is holding at December 2022.

payload.description: Coney Island Yard complex long-term flood mitigation and infrastructure project. 80% complete, within $521M budget, SC December 2022. New installation approach for traction power cables being monitored. Flood protection, drainage, signal work, new circuit breaker houses progressing well.

tool_args.label: 207th Street Yard & Shop Flood Mitigation
tool_args.raw_text: The project is currently 72% complete and C&D's EAC remains $633M. The project's earlier SC date of November 2023 is now forecast to be May 2024, a 6-month delay, due to late procurement and delivery of signal equipment.

payload.description: Flood mitigation at 207th Street Yard and Shop. 72% complete. EAC $633M. SC forecast May 2024 (6-month delay from Nov 2023). Delay due to late procurement and delivery of signal equipment. Signal contractor's performance must improve. Flood wall, track improvements and facilities making good progress.

tool_args.label: Clifton Shop
tool_args.raw_text: The project is nearing completion at 94%, and beneficial use has been achieved. SC date moved from June to October 2022. Budget $212M.

payload.description: Clifton Shop (SIR). 94% complete. Beneficial use achieved. SC moved from June to October 2022 (may be delayed additional 2 months). Budget $212M. Potential unforeseen hazardous materials during demolition of old shop as top risk.

tool_args.label: Bus Radio System (BRS)
tool_args.raw_text: The project, which is 67% complete, has exceeded its budget and has been significantly delayed. EAC of $330M exceeds current budget by $36M. IEC forecasts higher EAC of $350M.

payload.description: Bus Radio System replacement for NYCT and MTA Bus. 67% complete. EAC $330M exceeds budget by $36M. IEC forecasts EAC $350M. Significant delays due to slow contractor progress on base stations, pilot bus installations. Contractor shows May 2024 SC; C&D driving to December 2023 SC. 350 buses/month needed.

payload.description: MTA Capital Program Committee Meeting. Agenda includes Summary of Actions, Public Comments, Approval of Minutes, Committee Work Plan, C&D Capital Program Update (Signals and Train Control), C&D Safety Report, M/WBE/DBE/SDVOB Participation, Capital Program Status, C&D Procurements.

tool_args.label: Capital Program Committee Meeting March 28, 2022
tool_args.raw_text: MINUTES OF MEETING MTA CAPITAL PROGRAM COMMITTEE March 28, 2022 New York, New York 12:00 P.M.

payload.description: Minutes of the March 28, 2022 Capital Program Committee meeting. Chair Janno Lieber called meeting to order at 12:41 PM. Four public speakers. Minutes of January 24 and February 22 meetings approved. CPC Work Plan approved. President Torres-Springer opening remarks. Various C&D project updates. Fifteen procurement actions brought for approval.

tool_args.label: QBL-W CBTC Full Operation Achieved
tool_args.raw_text: The project has already placed CBTC into service on all sections, with the last section having been placed into operation February 2022.

payload.description: Full CBTC operation achieved on the entire Queens Boulevard Line West. Last section placed into operation February 2022. CBTC now in full operation on the entire QBL-W line.

tool_args.label: Grand Central - Times Square Shuttle ADA Completion
tool_args.raw_text: C&D recently achieved Substantial Completion (SC) on the Grand Central – Times Square Shuttle, which as a result of this work is now fully ADA accessible, easier to navigate and faster to board

payload.event_name: Grand Central - Times Square Shuttle ADA Completion
payload.description: Substantial Completion achieved on Grand Central - Times Square Shuttle. Now fully ADA accessible with new staircase entrance at 42nd Street and Broadway completed.

tool_args.label: 207th Street Sewer Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: SC originally February 2024, but due to third party utility company delays, as well as impact from a conflicting Department of Sanitation (DOS) moratorium, SC now January 2025, an 11-month delay. Project is over budget, with an EAC of $170M.

payload.description: Sewer relocation at 207th Street Yard. Original SC Feb 2024, revised to Jan 2025 (11-month delay). Third party utility delays and DOS moratorium. EAC $170M - over budget. Budget modification being prepared.

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge EAC $310M
tool_args.raw_text: The project is forecasting a final Estimate at Completion (EAC) of $310M

tool_args.label: QBL-W First Interoperable CBTC for NYCT
tool_args.raw_text: QBL-W is the first interoperable CBTC project implemented for NYCT, allowing trains with CBTC to run on the same line at the same time with carborne and wayside CBTC equipment from different suppliers (Siemens and Thales).

payload.claim_text: QBL-W is the first interoperable CBTC project implemented for NYCT, allowing trains with CBTC to run on the same line at the same time with carborne and wayside CBTC equipment from different suppliers (Siemens and Thales).

tool_args.label: 8th Ave CBTC First to Use Axle Counters
tool_args.raw_text: This project builds on the technology introduced in previous CBTC projects, notably the CBTC interoperability achieved on the QBL-W line, and (for the first time) introduced Axle Counters in lieu of Insulated Joints/Track Circuits to reduce life-cycle costs and maintenance.

payload.claim_text: The 8th Avenue CBTC project introduced Axle Counters in lieu of Insulated Joints/Track Circuits for the first time to reduce life-cycle costs and maintenance.

tool_args.label: 8th Avenue Line Structural Improvements (C48731)
tool_args.raw_text: Design-Build Services for Structural Improvements on the Eighth Ave Line. Repairs to structural defects, construction of new tunnel lighting room, installation of fiber optic cables on the 8th Avenue Line.

payload.description: Contract C48731 to JTTC JV (J-Track, LLC and TC Electric, LLC) for design-build services for structural repairs, new tunnel lighting room, and fiber optic cable installation on the 8th Avenue Line. Amount $51,600,000. 818 calendar days. Must coordinate with CBTC work under contract S48006.

tool_args.label: B&T Facilities in Sound Condition per FHWA
tool_args.raw_text: in accordance with FHWA ratings, these critical revenue-producing assets are in sound condition, which directly benefits the MTA's bond rating, thus lowering debt service costs and helping the MTA's overall financial position.

payload.claim_text: In accordance with FHWA ratings, MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities are in sound condition, benefiting MTA's bond rating, lowering debt service costs and helping the MTA's overall financial position.
